media.title: Knicks' first championship in 53 years a product of trust and sacrifice
media.description: Shortly before a historic playoff run, Knicks owner James Dolan spoke to his team and gave a message of sacrifice. Ten weeks later, and the New York Knicks are NBA champions.

items.title: Jalen Brunson last night:
items.author: Justin Kubatko
items.body_excerpt:
Jalen Brunson last night:

✅ 45 PTS
✅ 14-27 FG
✅ 4-7 3P
✅ 13-15 FT

Brunson is the fourth player to score at least 45 points in a Finals-clinching win.

He scored 47.9% of New York's points, the fifth-highest such figure in a Finals game.

Read, share, subscribe ⬇️

A heliocentric model can work.

For the Lakers the challenge isn't the star. It's finding the multidimensional supporting cast around him👇

items.title: Hollander POTD Sam Perkins. Drafted 4th in 84 by DAL+ fit in well (14 ppg 8 rpg). LAL signed him in 90 to big contract to help keep Showtime going. Traded to SEA in 93 where he was a proto-stretch 5 for great team. Finished up w/IND from 98-01. Went by "Big Smooth."

items.title: The Knicks dropped one measly game on the road in their entire run to the NBA championship (9-1). Nine straight wins on the road.
items.author: NBA Stat
items.body_excerpt:
The Knicks dropped one measly game on the road in their entire run to the NBA championship (9-1). Nine straight wins on the road.

Hard to fathom that the Atlanta Hawks was the team that gave them the most trouble.

#NBA

images.alt:
Prediction: Knicks in 6
To win a series without home-court advantage, you’ve got to get a game on the road. The Knicks have done that in eight of the 10 playoff series they’ve played in the Brunson era, including the last six. They enter this matchup with a significant rest advantage, with all five Spurs starters having played more minutes in these playoffs than any member of the Knicks. They won’t have the best player in the series; they’re also deeper in offensive talent than the Thunder team that just took the Spurs the distance without two of its three best creators.

Wembanyama is a wholly different challenge than anything the Knicks have yet to face. But the Knicks have spent the last month proving themselves capable of handling lots of different kinds of challenges, showcasing the kind of dynamism and versatility on both ends of the floor that makes them built to solve even the most complex problems.

I think they get one in San Antonio. I think they find just enough answers. And I think the result is … well, something that’s seemed unthinkable for most of the last 53 years.

items.title: The NBA championship.
items.author: Sam DiGiovanni
items.body_excerpt:
The NBA championship.
The NBA cup.
The greatest comeback win in Finals history.
Best point differential ever in a playoff run.
Most dominant 13-game stretch ever.

It’s all ours.
